title: HTML Frontend Junior Specialist Mock Test
description: Fundamental assessment for junior frontend developers covering semantic HTML5, accessibility (A11y), forms, SEO basics, and document structure.

questions.text: Which HTML5 element is most appropriate for wrapping a standalone piece of content that could be reused or redistributed (like a blog post or news story)?
questions.explanation: The <article> element is intended for self-contained content that makes sense on its own, independent of the rest of the site.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the 'alt' attribute in an tag?
options: To show a tooltip when hovering
To provide a description for screen readers and when the image fails to load
To define the alignment of the image
To link the image to another page
questions.explanation: Accessibility is key; 'alt' text is used by screen readers and displays if the image file is broken.

questions.text: In a form, which attribute connects a <label> to a specific <input>?
questions.explanation: The 'for' attribute on the label must match the 'id' attribute on the input to create a semantic connection.

questions.text: What does the <meta charset='UTF-8'> tag do?
options: Sets the language of the page
Specifies the character encoding for the document
Connects the HTML to a CSS file
questions.explanation: It tells the browser to use the UTF-8 character set, which covers almost all characters and symbols in the world.

questions.text: Which tag is used to create a numbered list?
questions.explanation: <ol> stands for 'Ordered List', which renders with numbers by default.

questions.text: Which attribute is used to open a link in a new tab?
questions.explanation: target='_blank' is the standard way to open a linked document in a new window or tab.

questions.text: Which element is used to define the main navigation links of a website?
questions.explanation: The <nav> element is a semantic tag intended specifically for blocks of navigation links.

questions.text: What is the correct way to add an external CSS file to an HTML document?
questions.explanation: External stylesheets are linked using the <link> tag inside the <head> section.

questions.text: Which input type is specifically for email addresses to provide basic validation?
questions.explanation: type='email' triggers mobile keyboard optimizations and basic format validation in modern browsers.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the <main> tag?
options: To contain all elements of the page including the header
To represent the dominant content of the <body>
To act as a parent for all navigation links
questions.explanation: There should only be one <main> element per page, containing the unique content of that document.
